Resolves #11685

Authored by GPT-5.5 (Codex Desktop). Session 019e44ba-d309-7e91-a819-36911fbf4e10.

FAIR-band: in-band [15/30]

Evidence: L2 (unit-simulated Chroma promote-window collision + full KB unit slice) -> L3 CI validation. Current-head CI executed the dockerized `integration-unified` suite successfully after the follow-up `ChromaNotFoundError` repair; local docker fixture command completed with both tests skipped after escalation.

Hardens the Knowledge Base shadow-swap path before activation. The canonical KB resolver now fails closed during an active live->parking / shadow->canonical promote window instead of using `getOrCreateCollection()` and risking an empty canonical collection. Failed pre-promote shadows are parked under a non-active recovery name. The default sync path remains unchanged; `NEO_KB_STALE_STRATEGY=shadow-swap npm run ai:sync-kb` is the explicit activation path.

## Deltas from ticket

- Implemented the no-canonical-window guard in `ChromaManager.getKnowledgeBaseCollection()` by resolving with `getCollection()`, detecting active swap artifacts, and only creating the canonical collection when no swap is in progress.
- Parked failed pre-promote shadow collections as `*-failed-shadow-*` instead of leaving active `*-shadow-*` names behind.
- Threaded `staleStrategy` through `DatabaseService` and added the explicit CLI env activation hook without changing the MCP `manage_knowledge_base` tool schema.
- Extended existing unit and dockerized integration coverage around the promote-window probe, plus the Chroma bootstrap `ChromaNotFoundError` fallback.
- Normalized tab indentation in the shadow-swap integration test after the approved review's non-blocking polish note.

- `npm run test-unit -- test/playwright/unit/ai/services/knowledge-base/ChromaManager.spec.mjs test/playwright/unit/ai/services/knowledge-base/VectorService.WorkVolumeBranching.spec.mjs test/playwright/unit/ai/services/knowledge-base/DatabaseService.sync.spec.mjs` -> 19 passed.
- `npm run test-unit -- test/playwright/unit/ai/services/knowledge-base/ChromaManager.spec.mjs` -> 7 passed.

- CI `integration-unified` first run at `d5b8337f5` failed with `ChromaNotFoundError: The requested resource could not be found`; fixed in `c51a3d01a`.
- CI on `c51a3d01a6a7f82619aed84b3532368cc21bea94` -> `lint-pr-body`, `check`, `unit`, `integration-unified`, `Analyze (javascript)`, and `CodeQL` passed. `integration-unified` passed in 6m33s.
- Review polish commit `73587ce9f` is whitespace-only in `KBBackupRestoreWipe.integration.spec.mjs`; `rg -nP '^\\t' test/playwright/integration/KBBackupRestoreWipe.integration.spec.mjs` found no remaining leading tabs.
- CI on `73587ce9ff4cd9f8a7a445df8e28d73a3f2411b3` -> `lint-pr-body`, `check`, `unit`, `integration-unified`, `Analyze (javascript)`, and `CodeQL` passed. `integration-unified` passed in 6m31s.
- `git diff --check` -> passed.

### ЁЯФм Depth Floor

**Challenge (per guide ┬з7.1):**

**Challenge 1 тАФ regex error-message matching is a fragile secondary classifier.** `#isCollectionNotFoundError` / `#isCollectionAlreadyExistsError` test `error.message` against `COLLECTION_NOT_FOUND_RE` / `COLLECTION_ALREADY_EXISTS_RE`. Error-message string matching is inherently brittle тАФ a chromadb wording change silently breaks the classification, and a misclassified not-found-vs-already-exists would route the resolver down the wrong branch. The `error.name === 'ChromaNotFoundError'` check is the robust primary; the regex is a fallback for the already-exists case (which has no typed-error check at all). Non-blocking тАФ but per ┬з5.1, an Empirical Isolation Test of what `chromadb@3.3.1` actually throws (error `name` + shape) for create-into-existing-name would let the already-exists path also key on a typed signal and shrink the regex surface.

**Challenge 2 тАФ orphaned swap artifacts produce a false-positive `KB_COLLECTION_SWAP_IN_PROGRESS`.** `#getActiveKnowledgeBaseSwapCollections` keys purely on the `{canonical}-parking-*` / `{canonical}-shadow-*` name prefixes. If a swap crashes *between* the two renames (leaving an orphaned `-parking-` collection with no live process), a later cold-cache resolve тАФ when the canonical is legitimately absent тАФ throws `KB_COLLECTION_SWAP_IN_PROGRESS` even though no swap is actually running. This fails **closed** (refuses rather than materializing a wrong empty canonical), which is the safe direction and operator-recoverable, so it is acceptable as shipped. But there is no reaper for orphaned `-parking-` artifacts тАФ FU-2's `failed-shadow` parking only covers the `embedChunks`-failure path, not a crash mid-promote-window. Worth a tracked follow-up if shadow-swap activation reveals it in practice.

**Rhetorical-Drift Audit (per guide ┬з7.4):**

- [x] PR description: "Deltas from ticket" accurately describes the diff тАФ fail-closed resolver, `failed-shadow` parking, `staleStrategy` threading, the `ChromaNotFoundError` repair. No overshoot.
- [x] Anchor & Echo summaries: new JSDoc reuses precise terminology (`@see #11685`), no metaphor overshoot.
- [x] `[RETROSPECTIVE]` tags: none authored in the PR.

**Findings:** Pass тАФ framing matches mechanical reality.

### ЁЯза Graph Ingestion Notes

*   **`[RETROSPECTIVE]`**: Beyond the three scoped follow-ups, this PR repairs a latent bug in the original #11684 `embedViaShadowSwap` rollback: the new `shadowPromoted` flag gates both the live-rollback (`liveParked && !shadowPromoted`) and the shadow-parking (`!shadowPromoted`). The original `if (liveParked)` would have attempted `liveCollection.modify({name: canonical})` even *after* the shadow already took the canonical name (e.g. a post-promote `count()` failure) тАФ a guaranteed name-collision on the rollback. Catching that while hardening the forward path is the right instinct: a correctness fix found by reasoning through the failure-state machine, not just patching the named FU items.
